rows.notes: Clear carried-over total at top right. Pounds '976', shillings '15', pence '10' and a clear halfpenny mark (written as 1/2). Vertical alignment with other totals confirms column boundaries.

Amount columns well aligned: 31 pounds, 0 shillings, 10 pence are distinct. Handwriting of the name is legible though the final letters of the first name are slightly flourished; context (list of names) supports this reading.
Abbreviated 'Do.' (ditto) then 'Beaton 2 yrs'. Numeric columns clear: 2 pounds, 14 shillings, 7 pence. Shillings/pence within valid ranges; handwriting of name is readable.

Amounts clear and well aligned: 36 pounds, 5 shillings, 6 pence. The personal name is moderately ambiguous in the middle letters (read as 'Tethrewin'); chosen reading based on letter shapes but could plausibly be spelled slightly differently.

Monetary columns are clear: 16 pounds, 0 shillings, 6 pence. The first name looks like 'Little Tex' (the letter could also be read as 'Tew' or 'Fox' by some readers); context (surname 'Taylor') makes 'Tex' plausible but there is some uncertainty in the first word.
Name 'Worley' is legible. Amount reads 20 pounds, 7 shillings, 4 pence and a clear farthing mark (written as '1/4'), interpreted as 0.25. Column alignment consistent with other rows.

Surname reads clearly as 'Kimber'. Amount columns read 10 pounds, 16 shillings, 7 pence with a three‑farthing mark ('3/4') after the pence, interpreted as 0.75. All numeric fields are within valid ranges.
Clear entry 'Eden'. Numeric alignment unambiguous: 7 pounds, 4 shillings, 0 pence. No fractional pence present.

Amounts read as 6 pounds, 17 shillings, 4 pence; columns are well aligned. The surname could be read as 'Hiorns' or 'Hiorns' due to the handwriting of the initial vowel/consonant cluster; chosen reading matches letter shapes observed but carries some uncertainty.
Name 'Hartley' is legible. Numeric columns read 1 pound, 19 shillings, 1 pence; shillings (19) and pence (1) are within valid ranges and clearly written.

Final boxed total at lower right. Pounds '1118', shillings '2', pence '4' and a clear halfpenny mark (1/2). Alignment with the vertical ruling lines and comparison with the carried-over total confirm column boundaries and values.
